__note: Hand-computed deterministic trace of dispatchFallback() applied to each of the 3 base Madrid fixtures from M2. Demonstrates how the orchestrator decides which fallback provider to call for each missing TIER-2 field.
scenario: After the demo-execution-trace.json runs (3 fixtures + 1 near-duplicate), each base fixture had outcome=fallback_required. Here is what the dispatcher emits.

dispatched_jobs.reason: tier2_gap_count:3 — Google authoritative for these (Tier-C with contact/geo boost)
tier2_gap_count:5 — hotel-website authoritative (Tier-B with year/MICE boost)

fallback_dispatches.critical_note: This row has the highest fallback urgency — TIER-1 is failing because total_rooms is missing AND only 4 amenity keys are determined.

dispatched_jobs.reason: tier2_gap_count:5 + RECOVERS total_rooms from hotel pages where Booking returned null
discipline_notes: HEAD probe first, GET only if robots allows AND domain authorised

expected_post_fallback_coverage.tier1_after: 12/12 (recovers total_rooms + brings amenities ≥5 once richer source returns canonical strings)
expected_post_fallback_coverage.outcome_projection: completed OR completed_with_warnings (depends on amenity recovery)

aggregate_dispatch_volume.wikidata_dispatch_note: Wikidata is reserved for year_opened cross-validation OR ownership_structure inquiry on institutional-tier hotels. The 3 sample fixtures have year_opened obtainable from hotel-website (Tier-B) at higher confidence than Wikidata (Tier-F). Wikidata only dispatches when hotel-website fails AND the hotel is gold-candidate.

discipline_summary.scraping_aggressiveness: ZERO. All 3 hotel-website jobs MUST pass robots.txt check, domain authorisation list (Phase 4 gate), HEAD-only probe, 4-8s jitter.
discipline_summary.no_blanket_crawls: true. There is no code path that emits more than one fallback job per provider per hotel per cycle.
discipline_summary.live_mode_gating: All 3 providers throw on live mode in Phase 1. Operator opt-in required per provider for Phase 4+.

institutional_interpretation: The dispatcher emits 2 jobs per Booking-incomplete hotel — one for Google Places (cheap, structured) and one for hotel-website (free, requires discipline).
Wikidata is dispatched separately as a batched discovery pass once per sweep, not per hotel.
Field routing is deterministic — same input produces same dispatch plan every time.
No field is dispatched to multiple providers in parallel — fallback chain is sequential by design, respecting the institutional tier order Booking → Google → Hotel website → Wikidata.
